column1: Table 306.10. Total fall enrollment in degree-granting postsecondary institutions, by level of enrollment, sex, attendance status, and race/ethnicity or nonresident status of student: Selected years, 1976 through 2023
Level of enrollment, sex, attendance status, and race/ethnicity or nonresident status of student
column13: Percentage distribution of U.S. resident students (excludes nonresident students)

column1: NOTE: Data in this table represent the 50 states and the District of Columbia. Prior to 2010, institutions were not required to report disaggregated data on students who were Asian, Pacific Islander, and of Two or more races, and each student could be counted in only one race category. For years prior to 1990, because of underreporting and nonreporting of racial/ethnic data, some figures are slightly lower than corresponding data in other tables. Data through 1990 are for institutions of higher education and U.S. service academies, while later data are for degree-granting institutions that participate in Title IV federal financial aid programs and U.S. service academies. Data for students whose race/ethnicity was unknown were prorated into these groups based on the reported racial/ethnic distribution by sex, level of enrollment, and attendance (does not include nonresident students). Degree-granting institutions grant associate’s or higher degrees. The degree-granting classification is very similar to the earlier higher education classification, but it includes more 2-year colleges and excludes a few higher education institutions that did not grant degrees. Race categories exclude persons of Hispanic ethnicity. Black includes African American, Hispanic includes Latino, and Pacific Islander includes Native Hawaiian. Some data have been revised from previously published figures. Detail may not sum to totals because of rounding.

SOURCE: U.S. Department of Education, National Center for Education Statistics, Higher Education General Information Survey (HEGIS), "Fall Enrollment in Colleges and Universities" surveys, 1976 and 1980; Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System (IPEDS), "Fall Enrollment Survey" (IPEDS-EF:90); and IPEDS, Fall Enrollment component, selected years, Spring 2001 through Spring 2023 (final data) and Spring 2024 (provisional data). (This table was prepared December 2024.)
